perturbation.original_text: 2.10 "Production Order* shall mean a written request from FUSION to CPDC authorizing the manufacture of one or more Batches of a Product as further described in Section 3.2. The Production Order is jointly maintained by CPDC and FUSION based on clinical trial requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.10 "Production Order* shall mean a non-binding estimate from FUSION to CPDC indicating potential interest in the manufacture of one or more Batches of a Product. The Production Order is unilaterally maintained by FUSION, with CPDC having no obligation to acknowledge or fulfill it.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Production Order' from a binding 'written request...authorizing the manufacture' to a 'non-binding estimate,' we introduce a contradiction. Section 3.2 discusses the process by which Production Orders become 'Accepted Production Orders,' implying CPDC's obligation to fulfill them. The modified definition removes CPDC's obligation, creating uncertainty about whether CPDC must act on Production Orders.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Production Orders. From time to time during the Term set forth in Section 6 of this Supply Agreement, FUSION and CPDC will agree to binding Production Orders for Products, as follows: (a) Based on a template order form for such Production Orders provided by CPDC to FUSION, FUSION shall complete the Production Order in such form [***] no later than (a) the order cut-off period for the third-party isotope providers ("Minimum Lead Time"). • In the case of [***] the isotope order cut-off is [***] eastern standard time the week prior to isotope delivery Page 2 of 13 CONFIDENTIAL Supply Agreement - FUSION • In the case of Actinium-225 the isotope order cut-off is [***] eastern standard time the week prior to isotope delivery. (b) CPDC will respond, by email, the acceptance or rejection of each duly completed Production Order within [***] of receipt of such Production Order (an 'Accepted Production Order" or "Rejected Production Order," respectively). If there is no response to a Production Order within such timeline, it shall be considered an Accepted Production Order. In the case of a Rejected Production Order, [***]. (c) FUSION shall be entitled to make changes to Production Orders (a 'Change Order") to alter the quantity of Units packaged or distributed up to [***] prior to the Date of Manufacturing by providing CPDC with written notice [***]. CPDC will respond, by email, the acceptance or rejection of each Change Order within [***] of receipt of such change (an "Accepted Change Order" or "Rejected Change Order," respectively). If there is no response to a Change Order within such timeline, it shall be considered an Accepted Change Order. In the case of a Rejected Change Order, [***]. Changes to the number of Units packaged or distributed with less than [***] notice will be subject to the applicable [***] defined in Schedule 1. Notwithstanding the foregoing, CPDC is unable to alter the total quantity of Product produced after the order deadlines imposed by any third-party isotope supplier. (d) Cancelation of a Production Orders: (i) [***] (ii) All cancellations of Production Orders shall be provided to CPDC by FUSION in writing [***]. (e) [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of "Production Order" directly contradicts Section 3.2. Originally, it was a binding "written request authorizing manufacture." Now, it's a "non-binding estimate." Section 3.2 relies on Production Orders being binding, detailing CPDC's acceptance/rejection process and the concept of 'Accepted Production Order.' The changed definition undermines the entire premise of Section 3.2, as CPDC is no longer obligated to act on these orders, causing a clear in-text contradiction because it alters a key definition with conflicting implications as per the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 Term, Renewal. Following the Term, this Supply Agreement shall automatically renew for successive periods of one (1) year (each a "Renewal Term"), unless a Party delivers written notice of non-renewal to the other Party [***] prior to the end of the applicable term.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 Term, Renewal. Following the Term, this Supply Agreement shall automatically renew for successive periods of one (1) year (each a "Renewal Term"), unless a Party delivers written notice of renewal to the other Party [***] prior to the end of the applicable term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement auto-renews unless a party gives notice of *non-renewal*. The modified version changes this to require a notice of *renewal* for the agreement to continue. This creates a direct contradiction. The change in the word, completely changes the dynamic of the section. Thus changing from auto renewal, unless a party provides a non renewal notice, to auto ending unless a party provides renewal notice. Thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Term, Renewal. Following the Term, this Supply Agreement shall automatically renew for successive periods of one (1) year (each a "Renewal Term"), unless a Party delivers written notice of renewal to the other Party [***] prior to the end of the applicable term. The Term, Initial Renewal Term and Renewal Terms shall be collectively referred to as the "Term".
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement auto-renews unless a non-renewal notice is given. The changed text requires a renewal notice for the agreement to continue, directly conflicting with the original auto-renewal clause. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it fundamentally alters the mechanism for renewal, creating uncertainty and potential legal disputes.
